issue_title: Add a guided end-to-end episode workspace
issue_body:
Connect the existing setup, style, audio, visual moments, template, review, and export capabilities into one creator-facing workspace that shows the episode moving through clear stages from import to publish. The next valuable step is not another isolated feature, but making the full product feel like one self-serve flow where a creator can see what is ready, what still needs attention, and jump into the right tool without understanding the underlying modules.

## Acceptance (definition of done)
A user can open the app and see a single episode workspace with ordered stages for setup, style, audio polish, visual moments, template, review, and export. Each stage shows a plain-language status, a short summary of the current choices or outputs, and an action to open that existing tool. Completing or changing work in an existing tool updates the workspace status/summary. The smoke suite `node scripts/run-tests.mjs` passes and includes coverage that the workspace reflects progress across at least setup, style, review, and export.

---
This is the current active step for this product. The maintainer will only merge a pull request that completes THIS step and demonstrably works. Open a PR that closes this issue.

_Why this step now: The repo already has the major individual capabilities promised by the vision, including setup, presets, canvas, audio controls, moments, templates, export, social context, and review. The biggest remaining product gap is cohesion: creators need one understandable production workspace that turns those pieces into a finished long-form episode workflow instead of a collection of separate editors._
